lines.text: We're {{ELAPSED}} in and the wind's shifted northwest. Caroline Weather calling it: temperatures dropping fast. You barricaded? Check your seals now, not at midnight.
The kill count's at 19 Madison-wide as of {{CLOCK}}. That's tracking about eleven percent below 2168 pace at this hour, which is statistically significant because—
Holden. Holden. The weather. People care about the weather when it's twenty-eight degrees and dropping.
Right. Yes. The northwest shift means the cold front that was forecast to arrive around 01:00 may actually clip us closer to 00:30. Caroline called that window originally.
I called 01:00 to 01:30. Don't give me 00:30. That's a different forecast.
The data suggests you should let me finish. Visibility is dropping. Anyone driving after midnight needs to understand that this isn't a clear night anymore. It's getting bad.
Which actually correlates with the lower kill count. Bad weather suppresses Purge activity. We saw that in 2162 during the ice storm. Lower visibility means fewer coordinated actions.
People staying in is good. People staying in is what we want.

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide confirmed kills: 19 ({{ELAPSED}} elapsed). East Side leading. Capitol Square cleared at 20:47.
Weather Alert: NW winds 12 mph, gusting to 18. Temperature 37°F and falling. Cold front arrival window now 00:15–01:00 (revised earlier estimate). Prepare now.
Text from 'Roof Tom,' Vilas Park: 'Gutters are rattling. Is that normal for wind or should I worry?' Caroline: Not normal. Get inside. Secure loose items NOW.
